hero.title: How to Measure
hero.subtitle: Accurate measurements are essential for custom-fit curtains and shades. Follow our step-by-step guides to ensure a perfect fit every time.

productTypes.title: Choose Your Product Type
productTypes.subtitle: Different products require different measurement approaches. Select the guide that matches your needs.

products.title: Curtains & Drapes
products.description: Measure for inside mount, outside mount, or ceiling mount installations. Includes fullness calculations for various pleat styles.

products.title: Roller & Roman Shades
products.description: Precise measurements for inside mount and outside mount shade installations. Critical depth requirements included.

curtainsGuide.title: Curtains Measurement Guide
curtainsGuide.subtitle: Follow these steps carefully for accurate curtain measurements.

steps.title: Determine Mount Type
steps.description: Decide between inside mount (within window frame) or outside mount (wall above window). Outside mount is recommended for better light control and to make windows appear larger.

tip.label: Tip:
tip.text: For outside mount, add 6-12 inches to window width for optimal coverage.

steps.title: Measure Width
steps.description: Measure the width of your window in three places: top, middle, and bottom. Use the widest measurement for outside mount, or the narrowest for inside mount.
items: Use a steel tape measure for accuracy

steps.title: Measure Height
steps.description: For floor-length curtains, measure from where the rod will be mounted to the floor. Subtract 1/2 inch for clearance, or add extra for a pooling effect.

steps.title: Calculate Fullness
steps.description: Different pleat styles require different fullness ratios:

shadesGuide.title: Shades Measurement Guide
shadesGuide.subtitle: Precise measurements are critical for custom shades. Follow these steps exactly.

steps.title: Check Window Depth (Inside Mount)
steps.description: Measure the depth of your window frame from the glass to the outer edge. You need at least 2 inches of depth for inside mount roller shades.

warning.label: Critical:
warning.text: Less than 2" depth requires outside mount.

steps.title: Measure Width
steps.insideMount: For inside mount: Measure the exact width of the window opening at the top, middle, and bottom. Record the narrowest measurement. Round down to the nearest 1/8".
steps.outsideMount: For outside mount: Measure the window width and add 3-4 inches to each side (6-8 inches total) for optimal light control.

steps.title: Measure Height
steps.insideMount: For inside mount: Measure the exact height of the window opening on the left, center, and right. Record the longest measurement.
steps.outsideMount: For outside mount: Measure from where you want the top of the shade to where you want it to end. Add 2-3 inches above the window frame for mounting.

steps.title: Note Obstructions
steps.description: Check for window cranks, handles, locks, or alarm sensors that might interfere with shade operation. Measure their depth if present.

commonMistakes.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
commonMistakes.subtitle: Learn from others' mistakes to ensure perfect measurements.

mistakes.title: Using a Cloth Tape
mistakes.description: Cloth measuring tapes can stretch and provide inaccurate measurements. Always use a rigid steel tape measure.

mistakes.title: Measuring Only Once
mistakes.description: Windows are rarely perfectly square. Measure width and height in multiple places to account for variations.

mistakes.title: Ignoring Depth Requirements
mistakes.description: Inside mount shades need adequate window depth. Always measure depth before choosing inside mount.

mistakes.title: Forgetting Obstructions
mistakes.description: Window handles, cranks, and locks can prevent proper operation. Always note these when measuring.

mistakes.title: Rounding Up Measurements
mistakes.description: For inside mount, always round down to the nearest 1/8". Rounding up can make the shade too large to fit.

mistakes.title: Measuring Old Treatments
mistakes.description: Never measure existing curtains or shades. Always measure the actual window opening for best results.

needHelp.title: Still Need Help?
needHelp.description: Our measurement experts are here to assist you. Schedule a free video consultation or send us photos of your windows for personalized guidance.
